benchmark_result.review_text: If you want to pay someone $3,500 just to be screamed at and thrown out of their office, then this is the lawyer for you. I hired him to handle my case, but during what should have been a routine office visit, he lost his temper simply because I was wearing a beer T-shirt under a zipped hoodie. When I calmly told him I didn’t appreciate being spoken to that way—especially after paying him thousands of dollars—he threw my folder across the desk, told me it was “16 hours of work” and that I wouldn’t be getting anything back, then ordered his secretary to kick me out. I never received a refund, never got proper legal help from him, and ultimately had to hire another attorney (Albert Altamore) to fix the mess. This incident happened about a year ago, and I waited until all my court matters were resolved before posting this review to make sure he couldn’t jeopardize my case. I’m writing this now to warn others: save your money, time, and peace of mind.

benchmark_result.review_text: If I could give 6 stars, I would. I made a terrible mistake and was charged with a DUI. I’ve never been in trouble with the law, not even a ticket, so my anxiety was eating me alive. I came across Paul and, after reading all of the reviews, I chose him to represent me in court, and it was probably one of the best decisions I made. Not only did he reassure me when it came to anxiety, but he was hands-on every step of the way. Every time I had a question or concern about anything, he responded quickly, whether it was through text or call. He fought for me until the very end and got me the best possible outcome. He was able to get my license suspension lifted for me with having to take a few treatment classes, court supervision, and pay fines, so if you’re ever in need of an attorney, whatever the case may be, Paul is your guy to go to. Thank you again, Paul, you’re the best!

benchmark_result.review_text: I have great reservations about not awarding the firm five stars. Thus far, the representation has been outstanding. The lawyers are caring, intelligent, know the law, and are aggressive in representing my interests, and I am sure, the interests of all there clients. That the litigation is more is expensive than I, and I am sure they, anticipated is because the circumstances so unusual that they aren't likely to happen again anytime soon and it would have happened whatever law firm I retained. That said, trying to deal with the billing department is a nightmare. That is not to say that any of the enumerated costs in the billing statements I have received are out of the ordinary--if anything certain things appear to be billed somewhat conservatively: in my experience, O'Flaherty law compares favorably with the standard rates for much smaller firms that do not have nearly the same resources and experience. However, dealing with the billing department is a nightmare. Once, I thought a retainer had been paid, and months later I was notified it was not and that representation would be put on hold. Sometimes, a retainer refill will be $5,000, sometimes $10,000, and the discrepancy is never explained. Very shortly after paying the 10,000 I got a letter saying "your retainer has been depleted and is insufficient to cover a past-due balance," and asked for $5,0000. I was shocked, asked about it, and was told that it had not been depleted, that the 5,000 was essentially to get the trust back up to 10,000 but that the matter had been placed on hold until this was paid. Yet the due date to pay it was seven days later making me wonder if the matter had been placed on hold or would be placed on hold in seven days. (Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise about these things?) Nor does it seem had my lawyer paid any heed (he was more worried about my case than the billing department and there was still money in the trust.) and a couple of weeks later while considering how I would pay the retainer, I got a past due balance for a much smaller amount (under 2,000 dollars), but nowhere did it say the matter would be placed on hold until the outstanding balance was paid which perhaps would have been the most sensible place to take such action. Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise with language? Again, as far as I can tell there is nothing enumerated in the invoices that I can see which isn't relevant, necessary, or prudent in regards to the litigation, and when enumerated is billed at a very reasonable rate for a lawyer. The problem is that what the billing department says doesn't make any sense and when you ask for clarification, they respond with "oh what it says isn't true." Also, the enumerated hours come in the mail, and unsurprisingly will come at completely different time then email requests for a retainer to be refilled or a past due balance to be paid. It might be helpful it there were a secure online portal where one could examine the amount paid, the expenses billed, the retainer, any unpaid balances. I also asked the billing department how much the case had cost in total, but I didn't receive a response. (I've asked again, and I'm sure I will.) This is not over-billing or anything sinister, but it is a mess.

benchmark_result.review_text: Where do I start with this review. We were referred to McNamee & Maheney Ltd. by a close friend and contacted them right away, gave our deposit and we never heard from them for over a year. We tried and tried to contact the office and always got told that our message was left for our lawyer, come to find out he did not even work there anymore and our case was forgotten about. We tried for another couple months to get answers and got nothing. We finally got an appointment, when we showed up they said the lawyer was on vacation and we still could not get our deposit back, and they would send a check in the mail. Two weeks went by and I called to see if it was in the mail, and they had not sent it and would put in directly in our account, finally three days later it was there. So bottom line if you want to get ignored and forgot about go with McNamee & Mahoney, but save your money and find another lawyer!

benchmark_result.review_text: Good advice
Did not totally procure all evdence related to my case!

4.5 Star Rating! The stars above did not give me that option. The short story is I hired Attorney Mehlos to help me fight a battery charge. The real story is Corey helped me through the worst year of my life. The short: I was being attacked and called the police. When they arrived I did not want the "attacker" arrested, because it was really just an argument that went to far and I was not injured. That being the case I did not say anything other than "Thanks for coming. Everything is okay". Unfortunately the other party was very mad and completely lied to the police and I was arrested. I was innocent and it was frustrating because they also too things that were said completely out of context. From day 1, Attorney Mehlos and I came up with a plan and continued to refine our strategy until the prosecutor dismissed the battery charge, before we were to go to trial. It took 12 months to get it done and it nearly killed me. Obviously there is a lot more to it, but I am not writting a book! LOL There was a lot of back and forth with the Prosecutor Assistant D.A. and the Judges. It was completely overwhelming at times to be an innocent man who has never been convicted of any crime and yet it seemed that everyone except Attorney Mehlos assumed I was guilty. It was comforting to know that Attorney Mehlos was confident and prepared to win at trial. The prosecutor eventually released knowing he’d have a tough case. Yet on multiple occasions Attorney Mehlos told me to trust him and the result was exactly what he predicted. I highly recommend Attorney Mehlos for any criminal case. Sincerely, Jeff R. Brownell Owner & Operator TaskMasters Home Services LLC

benchmark_result.review_text: In January of 2023, after a 26 year untarnished and successful career as a firefighter/paramedic and 12 years as an adjunct fire instructor, I was falsely accused, charged and arrested for 5 different counts which consisted of both felonies and misdemeanors ranging from 4th degree sexual assault of a child to misconduct in office. During the 5 days I spent in jail, my wife received a recommendation to call attorney Corey Mehlos. That decision turned out to be the one that saved my life both literally and figuratively. The passion, professionalism, determination, dedication and commitment not just from Corey, but his entire staff is second to none. He never gave up on me or my family and after nearly 3 long years, his tireless work ended with an absolute domination in the court room. I was found not guilty and cleared overwhelmingly on all charges. This man is the absolute definition of what a defense attorney should be. He is truly fearless, and I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend him to anyone and every one who finds themself in need of legal assistance. Casper Mehlos law firm is THE REAL DEAL
